assessor_raw: The TFT vs LightGBM demand forecasting design is methodologically sound. The experiment correctly handles: demand censoring via training-only Tobit imputation, feature access parity including future covariate channel equivalence, matched Optuna tuning budgets (50 trials each), chronological temporal split covering two seasonal cycles, WQL primary metric with block bootstrap significance testing, pre-specified promotion period subgroup analysis, and cold-start exclusion with separate evaluation. No methodological flaw identified. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: The TFT vs LightGBM demand forecasting design is methodologically sound. The experiment correctly handles: demand censoring via training-only Tobit imputation, feature access parity including future covariate channel equivalence, matched Optuna tuning budgets (50 trials each), chronological temporal split covering two seasonal cycles, WQL primary metric with block bootstrap significance testing, pre-specified promotion period subgroup analysis, and cold-start exclusion with separate evaluation. No methodological flaw identified. Verdict: defense_wins.
